label: Code edit loop
description: Read buggy code, identify the causal failure, produce a minimal correct edit, verify against test. This is the first depth where causal structure is unambiguous — an edit is an intervention, a test is causal feedback.

bugs.utterance: Fix the off-by-one error in the sum_to_n function — it should include n in the sum
bugs.causal_note: range(n) produces 0..n-1, missing n. The fix changes the range bound — a one-token causal intervention.

bugs.utterance: Fix the NoneType error in get_user_email — handle the case where user is None
bugs.buggy_code:
def get_user_email(user):
    return user['email']
bugs.causal_note: The bug is assuming user is always a dict. The fix adds a null guard — adding a conditional branch changes the control flow.

bugs.utterance: Fix the discount logic — items over 100 should get 10% off, not under
bugs.causal_note: Wrong comparison operator changes which branch executes. The fix is a single character change (< to >=) with a different causal meaning.

bugs.utterance: Fix divide — it crashes on zero division. Return None when divisor is 0
bugs.buggy_code:
def divide(a, b):
    return a / b
bugs.causal_note: Missing edge case for zero divisor. The fix adds a guard clause — one new conditional branch.

bugs.utterance: Fix the total calculation — it's using the wrong variable for the quantity
bugs.causal_note: The wrong variable (prices instead of quantities) gives a wrong total. The fix changes one identifier — a semantically minimal change with a large effect.

bugs.utterance: Fix is_even — it returns True for odd numbers
bugs.buggy_code:
def is_even(n):
    return n % 2 == 1
bugs.expected_fix:
def is_even(n):
    return n % 2 == 0
bugs.causal_note: Boolean logic inverted. One character change with exact opposite causal meaning.

bugs.utterance: Fix append_to_list — it mutates the default argument across calls
bugs.causal_note: Mutable default argument is a classic Python bug. The fix changes the causal structure of the default — from shared mutable state to fresh list per call.

bugs.utterance: Fix first_element — it should return None, not raise, on empty list
bugs.buggy_code:
def first_element(lst):
    return lst[0]
bugs.causal_note: No guard for empty list. Fix adds a conditional check — control flow change.

bugs.utterance: Fix concat — it fails when one of the inputs is not a string
bugs.buggy_code:
def concat(a, b):
    return a + b
bugs.expected_fix:
def concat(a, b):
    return str(a) + str(b)
bugs.causal_note: Type confusion — + works on str+str and int+int but fails on str+int. Fix wraps both in str().

bugs.utterance: Fix greet — the default greeting should be 'Hello', not 'Hi'
bugs.buggy_code:
def greet(name, greeting='Hi'):
    return f'{greeting}, {name}'
bugs.expected_fix:
def greet(name, greeting='Hello'):
    return f'{greeting}, {name}'
bugs.causal_note: Wrong default parameter value. Minimal lexical fix with semantic effect.

bugs.utterance: Fix the loop in sumArray — it should include the last element
bugs.causal_note: Off-by-one: arr.length - 1 skips the last element. Fix removes the -1.

bugs.utterance: Fix getName — it crashes when obj is null
bugs.causal_note: Optional chaining fixes the null reference. One operator change with a large causal effect.

bugs.utterance: Fix canAccess — users under 18 should not have access
bugs.causal_note: Wrong comparison. > excludes 18, >= includes it. One character, different causal boundary.

bugs.utterance: Fix getFirst — return undefined for empty arrays
bugs.causal_note: No empty-check guard. Fix adds an early return conditional.

bugs.utterance: Fix the type annotation — the function returns a string, not a number
bugs.causal_note: Wrong return type annotation. The fix changes number to string — the correct causal type.

bugs.utterance: Fix getConfig — handle the case where config is undefined
bugs.causal_note: No undefined guard. Fix adds optional chaining with nullish coalescing.

bugs.utterance: Fix the validation — it should reject empty strings, not accept them
bugs.test: assert validate('') == False; assert validate('hi') == True
bugs.causal_note: Boolean branches swapped. The fix inverts the return values — same condition, different causal mapping.

bugs.utterance: Fix the area calculation — it should use radius, not diameter
bugs.causal_note: Wrong variable name. The function expects diameter but divides by 2 to get radius. Better to just take radius directly — removes unnecessary computation.

bugs.utterance: Fix safe_divide — handle both zero division AND type errors
bugs.causal_note: Handles zero division but not type errors. Fix adds a try/except — one additional causal branch for a different failure mode.

bugs.utterance: Fix isLeapYear — years divisible by 100 are not leap years unless also divisible by 400
bugs.causal_note: Incomplete leap year logic. Missing the century rule and the 400-year exception. Fix adds two additional conditional branches.

bugs.utterance: Fix the access check — it should deny access if both conditions are false, not if either is false
bugs.buggy_code:
def can_access(is_admin, is_verified):
    return is_admin or is_verified
bugs.expected_fix:
def can_access(is_admin, is_verified):
    return is_admin and is_verified
bugs.causal_note: Wrong boolean operator. OR allows access if either is true. AND requires both. Different causal rule for access.

bugs.utterance: Fix get_last — it crashes on empty list, return None instead
bugs.buggy_code:
def get_last(lst):
    return lst[-1]
bugs.causal_note: No empty check before indexing. Fix adds a guard clause.

bugs.utterance: Fix countdown — it never terminates because the loop variable is not decremented
bugs.causal_note: Missing decrement causes infinite loop. Fix adds n -= 1 — a single-line causal intervention.

bugs.utterance: Fix the counter — it loses updates under concurrent access
bugs.buggy_code:
counter = 0
def increment():
    global counter
    counter += 1
bugs.expected_fix:
import threading
lock = threading.Lock()
counter = 0
def increment():
    global counter, lock
    with lock:
        counter += 1
bugs.causal_note: Missing lock causes lost updates under concurrency. Fix adds a threading.Lock — synchronisation is a causal intervention on shared state.

bugs.utterance: Fix get_score — it uses 'name' instead of 'score' as the dictionary key
bugs.buggy_code:
def get_score(record):
    return record['name']
bugs.expected_fix:
def get_score(record):
    return record['score']
bugs.causal_note: Wrong dictionary key. One identifier change with different semantic meaning.

bugs.utterance: Fix the query — it is vulnerable to SQL injection via string interpolation
bugs.test: assert get_user("'; DROP TABLE users; --") is not None
bugs.causal_note: SQL injection via string interpolation. Fix uses parameterised query — the intervention is changing the causal flow of untrusted input into the query.

bugs.utterance: Fix cache — it grows unboundedly because there is no eviction policy
bugs.causal_note: Unbounded cache growth. Fix adds LRU eviction — a causal constraint on memory.

bugs.utterance: Fix the import — use json, not simplejson
bugs.causal_note: Wrong import. simplejson and json have different APIs. Fix changes the import source.

bugs.utterance: Fix check_password — the assertion is wrong, it should reject empty passwords
bugs.causal_note: assert raises an exception instead of returning False. Fix replaces assert with an if guard — different causal path.

bugs.utterance: Fix add_user — it overwrites existing users with the same ID instead of skipping
bugs.causal_note: Overwrites without checking. Fix adds an existence guard.

bugs.utterance: Fix the sum function — it misses the last element
bugs.causal_note: Off-by-one: len(nums)-1 skips last element. Fix removes the -1.

bugs.utterance: Fix getName — it panics when the pointer is nil
bugs.causal_note: Nil pointer dereference panics. Fix adds a nil guard — control flow change prevents the crash.

bugs.utterance: Fix first — it panics on empty slice, return zero value instead
bugs.causal_note: No bounds check before indexing. Fix adds a length guard.

bugs.utterance: Fix canVote — the age threshold should be 18 or older, not strictly over 18
bugs.causal_note: > excludes 18, >= includes it. One character difference in causal boundary.

bugs.utterance: Fix the counter — concurrent access corrupts the value
bugs.causal_note: Missing mutex causes data races. Fix adds sync.Mutex — synchronisation as causal ordering.

bugs.utterance: Fix sendMessage — it panics when the channel is nil
bugs.causal_note: Send on nil channel blocks forever. Fix adds a nil guard before the send.

bugs.utterance: Fix buildString — use strings.Builder instead of concatenation for efficiency
bugs.causal_note: String concatenation in a loop is O(n^2). Fix uses strings.Builder — a causal optimisation of allocation patterns.

bugs.utterance: Fix the sum function — it excludes the last element
bugs.causal_note: Off-by-one in range. Fix uses iter().sum() — idiomatic Rust avoids the manual index entirely.

bugs.utterance: Fix get_first — it panics on None, return default instead
bugs.causal_note: .unwrap() panics on None. Fix uses unwrap_or(0) — a safe causal fallback.

bugs.utterance: Fix the borrow — it clones unnecessarily causing performance issues
bugs.causal_note: Unnecessary clone for read-only access. Fix changes to &[String] — a borrow instead of move is a causal difference in ownership.

bugs.utterance: Fix the shared counter — it doesn't synchronise access
bugs.expected_fix: // code is correct — no fix needed, the Mutex is already used
bugs.causal_note: The code is actually correct. This is a distractor: the model must recognise the fix is already in place and not make unnecessary changes.

bugs.utterance: Fix find_user — it should return Option<String>, not Result
bugs.causal_note: Using Result where Option is appropriate. The fix changes the return type and error semantics — different causal type.

bugs.utterance: Fix remove_all — it only removes the first occurrence instead of all
bugs.test: // let mut v = vec![1,2,1,3]; remove_all(&mut v, 1); assert_eq!(v, vec![2,3])
bugs.causal_note: Removes only the first match. Fix uses retain() which removes all matching elements — different causal scope.

bugs.utterance: Fix double — it should double each element but returns the original
bugs.causal_note: Takes ownership unnecessarily. Fix changes to &[i32] — borrowing is a causal difference in ownership semantics.

bugs.utterance: Fix the loop in sumArray — it misses the last element
bugs.causal_note: Off-by-one: arr.length - 1 skips the last element.

bugs.utterance: Fix getName — it throws NullPointerException when user is null
bugs.causal_note: Null dereference causes NPE. Fix adds a null guard.

bugs.utterance: Fix canEnter — users under 18 should not be allowed
bugs.causal_note: > excludes 18, >= includes it.

bugs.utterance: Fix getLast — it crashes on empty array, return null instead
bugs.causal_note: No length check before indexing an empty array.

bugs.utterance: Fix buildString — use StringBuilder instead of + concatenation in a loop
bugs.causal_note: String concatenation in a loop creates O(n^2) copies. Fix uses StringBuilder — a causal optimisation.

bugs.utterance: Fix removeEven — it throws ConcurrentModificationException
bugs.causal_note: Modifying a list while iterating throws ConcurrentModificationException. Fix uses removeIf — a causal change in the iteration protocol.

bugs.utterance: Fix divide — it throws ArithmeticException on zero divisor
bugs.causal_note: Zero divisor causes ArithmeticException. Fix adds a guard clause.

bugs.utterance: Fix the sum function — it misses the last element
bugs.causal_note: Off-by-one: n - 1 skips the last element.

bugs.utterance: Fix getName — it segfaults when the pointer is null
bugs.causal_note: Null pointer dereference causes a segfault. Fix adds a null guard.

bugs.utterance: Fix copyBuffer — it writes past the end of the destination
bugs.causal_note: Buffer overflow: <= writes one past the end. Fix uses < instead of <=.

bugs.utterance: Fix createUser — it leaks memory, use a smart pointer
bugs.causal_note: Raw new without delete leaks memory. Fix uses unique_ptr — RAII as causal lifecycle management.

bugs.utterance: Fix removeAll — it removes while iterating incorrectly
bugs.causal_note: Erasing while iterating invalidates the iterator. Fix uses erase-remove idiom — a causal difference in iteration semantics.

bugs.utterance: Fix getMessage — it returns a dangling reference to a local variable
bugs.causal_note: Returning reference to a local causes undefined behaviour. Fix returns by value — a causal difference in lifetime.

bugs.utterance: Fix the base class — add a virtual destructor to prevent undefined behaviour on delete
bugs.test: // Base* b = new Derived(); delete b; // no leak
bugs.causal_note: Non-virtual destructor in base class causes undefined behaviour when deleting through base pointer. The fix is one keyword — virtual.

bugs.utterance: Fix the click handler — it loses the 'this' context
bugs.causal_note: Regular function loses 'this' context. Fix uses arrow function — lexical scoping of 'this' is a causal difference in execution context.

bugs.utterance: Fix getUserCity — it throws when address is undefined
bugs.causal_note: No optional chaining. Nested property access throws on undefined.

bugs.utterance: Fix sum — it concatenates instead of adding when inputs are strings
bugs.causal_note: + concatenates strings instead of adding. Fix casts both to Number — a causal type intervention.

bugs.utterance: Fix createCounters — each counter should track its own index
bugs.causal_note: var creates function-scoped variable, all closures share the same i. Fix uses let for block scoping — a causal difference in variable lifetime.

bugs.utterance: Fix getFirst — it returns undefined for empty arrays, should return null
bugs.causal_note: Returns undefined for empty array (implicit). Fix explicitly returns null — different causal default.

bugs.utterance: Fix the generic constraint — T should extend HasId, not be unconstrained
bugs.causal_note: Unconstrained any[]. Fix uses a generic constraint — a causal type narrowing.

bugs.utterance: Fix processInput — it doesn't narrow the union type before using string methods
bugs.causal_note: No type narrowing before calling string method on union type. Fix adds a typeof guard — a causal type check.

bugs.utterance: Fix getDisplayName — it doesn't handle null user
bugs.causal_note: No null guard on union type. Fix adds optional chaining with nullish coalescing.

bugs.utterance: Fix getValue — the index signature should allow string keys
bugs.causal_note: d.key accesses the literal 'key' property, not the dynamic key. Fix uses bracket notation — a causal difference in property access semantics.

bugs.utterance: Fix fetchData — it doesn't await the promise, returns a pending promise
bugs.causal_note: Returns a promise instead of awaiting. Fix makes the function async and uses await — a causal difference in async flow.

bugs.utterance: Fix get_name — it crashes when user is nil
bugs.causal_note: No nil guard. Fix uses safe navigation operator (&.) — a causal nil check.

bugs.utterance: Fix the sum method — it misses the last element
bugs.causal_note: Three-dot range (...) excludes the end. Two-dot range (..) includes it. One character causal difference.

bugs.utterance: Fix getName — it crashes when user is null
bugs.causal_note: No safe call on nullable type. Fix adds ?. and ?: — Kotlin's safe access operators.

bugs.utterance: Fix sumArray — it skips the last element
bugs.causal_note: Off-by-one with until. Fix uses built-in .sum() — idiomatic Kotlin avoids manual index.

bugs.utterance: Fix getName — it crashes when user is nil
bugs.causal_note: Force unwrap crashes on nil. Fix uses optional chaining and optional cast — safer causal unwrapping.

bugs.utterance: Fix sumArray — it misses the last element
bugs.causal_note: Off-by-one in half-open range. Fix uses reduce — idiomatic Swift.

bugs.utterance: Fix the query — it concatenates user input directly into SQL
bugs.causal_note: SQL injection via string concatenation. Fix uses parameterised query — the intervention is separating data from code.

bugs.utterance: Fix the comparison — NULL = NULL evaluates to NULL, not TRUE
bugs.buggy_code: SELECT * FROM users WHERE deleted = NULL
bugs.expected_fix: SELECT * FROM users WHERE deleted IS NULL
bugs.test: -- returns rows where deleted IS NULL
bugs.causal_note: NULL = NULL is NULL (not TRUE). Fix uses IS NULL — SQL's correct null comparison, a causal difference in evaluation semantics.

bugs.utterance: Fix the script — the variable is unquoted, causing word splitting
bugs.buggy_code:
backup_dir="/path/to/files"
rm -rf $backup_dir
bugs.expected_fix:
backup_dir="/path/to/files"
rm -rf "$backup_dir"
bugs.test: # rm -rf handles paths with spaces correctly
bugs.causal_note: Unquoted variable causes word splitting. Fix adds quotes — a causal difference in shell parsing.

bugs.utterance: Fix the script — user input is evaluated as a command
bugs.buggy_code:
filename=$1
cat $filename
bugs.expected_fix:
filename="$1"
cat -- "$filename"
bugs.test: # cat works on files with special characters
bugs.causal_note: Unquoted user input passed to cat. Fix quotes the variable and uses -- to prevent flag injection — a causal change in input handling.

bugs.utterance: Fix get_name — it errors when the table is nil
bugs.expected_fix:
function get_name(user)
    if user == nil then return nil end
    return user.name
end
bugs.causal_note: Nil table access errors. Fix adds a nil guard.

bugs.utterance: Fix sumArray — off-by-one in the loop
bugs.buggy_code:
function sumArray(arr)
    local total = 0
    for i = 1, #arr - 1 do
        total = total + arr[i]
    end
    return total
end
bugs.expected_fix:
function sumArray(arr)
    local total = 0
    for i = 1, #arr do
        total = total + arr[i]
    end
    return total
end
bugs.causal_note: #arr - 1 skips the last element.

bugs.utterance: Fix GetName — it throws NullReferenceException when user is null
bugs.causal_note: NullReferenceException on null access. Fix adds ?. null-conditional and ?? null-coalescing.

bugs.utterance: Fix SumArray — it skips the last element
bugs.causal_note: Off-by-one: arr.Length - 1 misses the last. Fix uses LINQ .Sum().

bugs.utterance: Fix the sum function — it skips the last element
bugs.causal_note: Off-by-one: $#_ - 1 skips the last index. Fix uses idiomatic Perl iteration.
